channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US imposes restrictions on 100 Nicaraguan officials after activist’s death
item.description: The death of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera has prompted calls from UN experts for an independent investigation.

item.title: Moment Israeli helicopter fires missile on Gaza City target
item.description: Footage shows the moment an Israeli helicopter launched a missile at an unknown target in the Gaza City neighbourhood.

item.title: WHO chief lauds Uganda’s response to Ebola outbreak
item.description: Tedros said the country had responded promptly and capably, but called for it to reopen the border with DR Congo.

item.title: Airline profits plummet as US fuel costs nearly double
item.description: IATA forecasts $350bn airline fuel bill for 2026, with profits dipping to their weakest margin since the COVID-19 years.

item.title: UN watchdog, Western nations call on Iran to restart nuclear cooperation
item.description: IAEA chief Rafael Grossi urges Tehran to re-engage. US and E3 demand information on whereabouts of uranium.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: the politics and profits of the beautiful game
item.description: The World Cup has yet to start, but politics, war, and controversy are already competing with football.

item.title: Nuclear risks rise as powers expand and modernise arsenals: SIPRI study
item.description: Nuclear states walking away from disarmament commitments amid heightened escalation dangers, leading institute warns.

item.title: US judge strikes down Trump’s $100,000 H1-B visa fee
item.description: In a ruling on Monday, a federal judge said the fee was a tax that Trump did not have the authority to impose.

item.title: Race tied between left- and right-wing rivals in Peru’s presidential vote
item.description: The split illustrates deep political polarisation in the South American country.

item.title: Three rescued, one killed in Russian drone strike on Ukraine block
item.description: Three rescued, one killed in Russian drone strike on Ukraine block.

item.title: 7.8 magnitude earthquake leaves dozens dead in the Philippines
item.description: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck the southern Philippines off the island of Mindanao, killing at least 32.

item.title: How Lebanon became the breaking point for the Iran war ceasefire
item.description: Iran has switched from projecting power via its proxies to using its own firepower to protect them, analysts say.

item.title: ‘Strategic doctrine’: Iran hails military shift after Beirut raid response
item.description: Iranians describe attacks on Israel as part of military approach prioritising 'taking initiative and offensive power'.

item.title: Lebanon’s children face physical, psychological trauma amid Israel’s war
item.description: Experts warn of lasting trauma for Lebanon's children as war continues to shatter their sense of safety.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel pushes deeper as conflict escalates
item.description: The latest developments from southern Lebanon amid escalating Israeli attacks.

item.title: ‘Lives turned in a second’: Family of baby Sam, shot dead by Israel, grieve
item.description: A father speaks of the moment his seven-month-old Palestinian son was shot dead in the West Bank by an Israeli soldier.

item.title: IAEA chief says Iran-US nuclear talks in ‘complicated phase’
item.description: IAEA chief says Iran-US nuclear talks in 'complicated phase'.

item.title: Why was Israel spared scrutiny for the 1967 USS Liberty attack?
item.description: US Representative Massie set to honour the crew of the vessel on the House floor, bringing attention to the 1967 attack.

item.title: Meta to take legal action against Israeli spyware company NSO
item.description: WhatsApp disrupted phishing attempts linked to NSO, blacklisted by the US for security concerns.

item.title: How extreme weather and heat could affect players at World Cup 2026
item.description: Climate experts warn that 25% of World Cup games could be played in very hot conditions, affecting fans and players.

item.title: Texas-bound jet crashes during emergency landing in Dominican Republic
item.description: Texas-bound private jet crashes during emergency landing in Dominican Republic.

item.title: Al Jazeera explains China-North Korea ties as Xi visits Pyongyang
item.description: China’s Xi Jinping is visiting North Korea for the first time in seven years.

item.title: At least 11 killed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir clashes before rally
item.description: Police crack down down on supporters of the Joint Awami Action Committee, which plans to hold a rally Tuesday.

item.title: The retaking of Cuba
item.description: Decades-old property claims, frozen since the 1960s, are being turned into live instruments of reconquest.

item.title: North Korea needs China for survival: Why does Beijing need Pyongyang?
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping visits North Korea as Beijing aims to boost ties amid growing Pyongyang-Moscow relations.
